original: Hawaiian surfs on the highest wave ever - a 50-foot tidal wave
NASA announces the four astronauts for 2024 Artemis II lunar mission around the Moon - Christina Hammock Koch, Victor Glover, Reid Wiseman and Canadian astronaut Jeremy Hansen
1st atomic powered spacecraft (snap) launched
1st airplane flight over Mt Everest
Start of the Pony Express, mail is delivered by horse and rider relay teams between St. Joseph, Missouri and Sacramento, California
Don Perry climbs a 20' rope in under 2.8 seconds (AAU record)
Wood block alarm invented, when alarm rang, it dropped 20 wood blocks
Conventional train world speed record: a French TGV train on the LGV Est high speed line sets new official world speed record - 574.8 km/h (357 mph)
First roster of Silver Bullets is announced, an all-female professional baseball team
Soviet Luna 10 completes its first orbit of the Moon
Matt Smith's first appearance as the 11th Doctor withKaren Gillanas his companion in BBC program "Doctor Who" during "The Eleventh Hour" episode
Science fiction film "Planet of the Apes", starringCharlton Hestonand Roddy McDowell, opens nationally in the United States
The Beatles hold top 6 spots on the record chart in Sydney, Australia
(Virgil) "Gus" Grissom, US Air Force test pilot and NASA astronaut (Mercury-Redstone 4; Gemini 3; Apollo 1), born in Mitchell, Indiana (d. 1967)
Soyuz T-11 carries 3 cosmonauts (1 Indian-Rakesh Sharma) to Salyut 7
Second flight of a liquid-fueled rocket byRobert Goddard
Highest mountain in North America, Alaska's Mt McKinley claimed to have been 1st climbed by 4 local men
Thomas Bos skates world record 3 km (3:65.16)
First mobile phone call is made in downtown Manhattan, NY, by Motorola employee Martin Cooper to Bell Labs headquarters in New Jersey
Hudsonville-Standale Tornado: The western half of the Lower Peninsula of Michigan is struck by a deadly F5 tornado
London's Nightingale hospital opened byPrince Charles(remotely) after nine days with 4,000 beds to treat COVID-19 patients at the ExCeL Centre
Then longest North American hockey game requires a 1:44:46 overtime as Maple Leaf Ken Doraty scores to beat Canadiens 1-0
St Francis Fighting Saints scores college baseball run record 71-1
Chevron Championship Women's Golf, Mission Hills CC: American Jennifer Kupcho wins her first LPGA tournament by 2 strokes over Jessica Korda
ICC Women's Cricket T20 World Cup, Eden Gardens, Kolkata: Hayley Matthews with 66 from 45 balls leads West Indies to 149/2; beat Australia 148/5 by 4 wickets with 2 balls to spare
Mario Lemieuxwins NHL scoring title, stopping Gretzky's 7 year streak
Bill Elliottsets NASCAR qualify record of 212.809 mph at Talladega
Margaret "Daisy" Ashford, British child author who wrote "The Young Visiters" (Mister Salteena's Plan) aged 9, born in Petersham, Surrey (d. 1972)
Éva Székely, Hungarian swimmer (Olympic gold 200m breaststroke 1952; silver 1956; first WR 400m individual medley 1953), born in Budapest, Hungary (d. 2020)
Wesley Brown, 1st African American US Naval Academy graduate, born in Baltimore, Maryland (d. 2012)

Fazlur Rahman Khan, Bangladeshi-American structural engineer and architect (Sears Tower, Chicago; John Hancock Center, Chicago; Metrodome, Minneapolis), born in Dhaka, British India (now Bangladesh) (d. 1982)
Vitaliy Davidov, Soviet ice hockey player (Olympic gold 1964, 68, 72), born in Moscow
Stella Chiweshe, Zimbabwean musician and storyteller (Africa’s best-known female mbira player), born in Mujumi Village, Mhondoro, Rhodesia (d. 2023)
Richard Thompson, English singer-songwriter, and guitarist ("Tear-Stained Letter"; "1952 Vincent Black Lightning"), born in Notting Hill Gate, London
Baltimore Orioles pull their 1st triple play (3-6-2 vs KC Athletics)
MLB Chicago Cubs trade Dennis Eckersley to Oakland A's for 3 minor league players
CricketerRahul Dravidmakes his international debut for India in an ODI against Sri Lanka
World War II: Dutch city of Hengelo freed from Nazi control by the Canadian Army
US PresidentHarry Trumansigns the Marshall Plan to rebuild war-torn Western Europe after World War II, granting an initial $5 billion in aid to 16 European countries
Istanbul's Ottoman Topkapi Palace is converted into a museum on orders ofMustafa Kemal Atatürk
Elvis Presleyrecords ‘It's Now Or Never’, ‘Fever’ and ‘Are You Lonesome Tonight’ at RCA studios in Nashville, Tennessee
The Temptations score their second US No. 1 with "Just My Imagination (Running Away With Me)"

Rock for the Rainforest benefit concert held at Carnegie Hall, NYC; performers include:Sting,Elton John,James Taylor,Jennifer Hudson,Rosanne Cash, Vince Gill, Esperanza Spaulding,Bruno Mars, Bryn Terfel, andBill Clinton
Frank Gotch wins world heavyweight wrestling championship in 2 hrs
Union forces occupy Confederate capital of Richmond & Petersberg, Virginia
Jane M Byrne (D), elected 1st woman mayor of Chicago, Illinois
San Antonio coach Geg Popovich is ejected after an NBA record low 63 seconds in the Spurs 113-85 loss in Denver; receives 2 technical fouls in a verbal confrontation with a referee
Australia formally adopts the United Nations Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples
John Burroughs, American writer and nature enthusiast (Burroughs Medal namesake), born in Delaware County, New York (d. 1921)
Matthew Ricketts, 1st African American man elected to Nebraska State Legislature, born in Henry County, Kentucky (d. 1917)
Michael Woodruff, English surgeon and scientist (organ transplantation), born in Mill Hill, London (d. 2001)
George Edwards, English naturalist and ornithologist (father of British ornithology), born in Stratford, Essex, England (d. 1773)
Mark Catesby, English naturalist and painter (Natural History of Carolina, Florida and the Bahama Islands), born in Essex, England (d. 1749)
James "Bubber" Miley, American jazz trumpet and cornet player (Duke Ellington, 1923-29 - "East St. Louis Toodle-Oo"; "Black And Tan Fantasy"), born in Aiken, South Carolina (d. 1932)
(Edward Elzear) "Zez" Confrey, American jazz pianist and composer ("Kitten on the Keys"; "Dizzy Fingers."), born in Peru, Illinois (d. 1971)
Mistinguett [Jeanne Florentine Bourgeois], French actress and singer (Moulin Rouge star), born in Enghien-les-Bains, Val-d'Oise, France (d. 1956)
Jimmy Matthews, Australian cricket spin bowler (8 Tests, 16 wickets; Victoria; only player to take 2 hat-tricks in same Test 1912), born in Mount Gambier, Australia (d. 1943)
(Arthur) "Dooley" Wilson, American piano player, singer, and actor (Casablanca; Stormy Weather; Beulah), born in Tyler, Texas (d. 1953)
Walter "Fats" Pichon American jazz pianist, singer, and big bandleader, born in New Orleans, Louisiana (d. 1967)
Jim Parker, American NFL football player guard and tackle (Baltimore Colts, 2 x NFL Champion, 8 x Pro Bowl), born in Macon, Georgia (d. 2005)
